name: Company and Business Entity Incorporation Guide

whoNeedsIt.value: Formal business organisations seeking registration and legal recognition in Zimbabwe.

documents.note: No current DCIP document checklist was available from a resolving official source on the review date.

prerequisites.note: The reviewed official service page does not publish entity-specific prerequisites.

value.taxTreatment: Not stated in the cited regulation
Not stated in the cited regulation

officialFees.note: No current official DCIP fee schedule was available from a resolving source during PB-000C. PB-002B located S.I. 95 of 2023 on the official ZIDA eRegulations portal; fees are entity-specific and must be rechecked for later amendments before payment.

processingTime.note: No current authority-published processing time was available from a resolving source.

renewalCycle.note: The reviewed official page does not state a renewal rule for the incorporation itself.

value.action: Apply for a company-name search.
Obtain the reserved company name.
Apply for company registration using the reserved name.
Obtain the company-registration documents after approval.

steps.note: The reviewed Ministry service page establishes DCIP's registration function but does not publish a current application sequence. PB-002B added the ordered company-registration sequence published by the official ZIDA eRegulations portal.

notes: Select the legal-entity form with DCIP and obtain the current checklist before filing.

name: ZIMRA Taxpayer Registration Guide

whoNeedsIt.value: Persons carrying on trade or business in Zimbabwe, including individuals and organisations registering through TaRMS.

documents.note: ZIMRA instructs applicants to attach supporting documents but the reviewed page does not publish one complete document list for every taxpayer type.

value: An email address and mobile number for TaRMS account activation and verification
The applicant's taxpayer type and identifying particulars

value.taxTreatment: Not applicable

officialFees.note: The previously reviewed registration guidance did not state a registration fee. PB-002B located ZIMRA's explicit statement that tax registration is free of charge.

processingTime.note: The reviewed official registration guidance does not state an approval time.

renewalCycle.note: The sources describe registration and issue of a TIN but do not publish a periodic renewal cycle.

value.action: Open TaRMS, choose Sign Up and create the user account.
Activate the account using the email sent by TaRMS, then sign in and complete the verification-code step.
Choose the applicable taxpayer route and complete the registration forms.
Attach the supporting documents requested for that taxpayer type and submit for ZIMRA approval.
After approval and TIN issue, sign in and complete the bank-account details requested by ZIMRA.

notes: Attachments vary by taxpayer type; use the live TaRMS prompts and ZIMRA guidance.

name: ZIMRA Value Added Tax Registration Guide

whoNeedsIt.value: Persons making taxable supplies who meet the current compulsory registration rules, and qualifying persons applying for voluntary VAT registration.

value: Sales schedule for the preceding twelve months for a compulsory-registration application, or projected sales schedule for the following twelve months for a voluntary-registration application
Supporting attachments requested by TaRMS for the completed VAT application
documents.note: The official page identifies the sales schedule but does not enumerate every possible supporting attachment.

value: An existing TaRMS taxpayer profile
A determination whether the application is compulsory or voluntary and whether supplies are local or exports

value.taxTreatment: Not applicable

officialFees.note: The previously reviewed VAT-registration guidance did not state a registration fee. PB-002B located ZIMRA's explicit statement that tax registration is free of charge.

processingTime.value: Within one working day after the taxpayer meets ZIMRA's VAT-registration requirements.
processingTime.note: The previously reviewed VAT-registration guidance did not state an approval time. PB-002B located the one-working-day service standard in ZIMRA's client charter.

renewalCycle.note: The reviewed sources do not publish a periodic renewal rule for VAT registration.

value.action: Sign in to TaRMS and open Taxpayer Profile, Tax Type, then New Tax Type and VAT.
Select local or export supplies, the effective date, and compulsory or voluntary registration.
Complete the taxable-turnover, assets and other application fields.
Attach the applicable sales schedule and the supporting documents requested by TaRMS.
Submit the application and obtain the VAT certificate if ZIMRA approves it.

notes: No turnover threshold is reproduced; confirm the current compulsory and voluntary rules with ZIMRA.

name: ZIMRA PAYE Employer Registration Guide

whoNeedsIt.value: A business that becomes an employer and must register for PAYE with ZIMRA.

documents.note: No PAYE-specific attachment checklist was located in the reviewed official guidance.

prerequisites.note: The reviewed official page establishes the employer obligation but does not state a complete prerequisite set for the application.

value.taxTreatment: Not applicable

officialFees.note: The previously reviewed PAYE guidance did not state a registration fee. PB-002B located ZIMRA's explicit statement that tax registration is free of charge.

processingTime.value: Within one working day after the taxpayer meets ZIMRA's PAYE-registration requirements.
processingTime.note: The previously reviewed PAYE guidance did not state an approval time. PB-002B located the one-working-day service standard in ZIMRA's client charter.

renewalCycle.note: The source does not publish a periodic renewal rule for PAYE employer registration.

steps.note: No PAYE-specific online application sequence was located in the reviewed official guidance.

notes: PAYE registration is separate from ongoing deduction, remittance and employee-return obligations.

name: NSSA Employer Registration Guide

whoNeedsIt.value: Employers of labour covered by NSSA's compulsory schemes.

documents.note: The self-service flow includes a company-documents upload but the reviewed official pages do not expose one complete document list.

value: Main employer details, including legal and trade names, business activity, company type and industry code
Employment start information and workforce and wage-bill particulars
At least one director and one contact person in the online application

officialFees.note: No employer-registration fee was stated in the reviewed official guide or registration flow.

processingTime.value: Within three days after NSSA receives complete and accurate employer-registration documents.
processingTime.note: No approval time was stated in the PB-000C sources. PB-002B located NSSA's 2023 service-delivery standard; confirm at review that the three-day standard remains current before customer-facing reliance.

renewalCycle.note: The sources describe employer registration and change notifications but do not publish a periodic renewal cycle.

value.action: Open NSSA self-service and start the employer-registration flow.
Complete the main employer, employment and workforce details.
Add the required director and contact-person details.
Upload the company documents requested by the live portal and submit the application.
Use the NSSA employer identifiers issued after verification for subsequent employer and employee returns.

notes: Confirm scheme coverage and the live portal's attachment list for the employer's circumstances.

name: PRAZ Bidder and Contractor Registration Guide

whoNeedsIt.value: Suppliers, bidders and contractors seeking eligibility to participate in Zimbabwe public procurement.

value: Memorandum and articles of association or other constitutive document
Certificate of incorporation
List of directors
Head-office and local physical-address particulars
Zimbabwean and foreign shareholding particulars
An eGP account and completed supplier business profile
Selection of the correct supplier category or categories

value.description: Local micro enterprise annual category registration
value.taxTreatment: Not stated on the cited portal

value.description: Local small or medium enterprise annual category registration
value.taxTreatment: Not stated on the cited portal

value.description: Other local enterprise annual category registration
value.taxTreatment: Not stated on the cited portal
Not stated on the cited portal

officialFees.note: The official portal also displays local-currency equivalents that can change with the stated exchange rate; confirm the payable amount at checkout.

processingTime.value: PRAZ states that it verifies payment and reviews the business profile within 48 hours at the relevant eGP review stage.
processingTime.note: This is the published review-stage timing, not a guarantee of end-to-end completion where corrections are required.

renewalCycle.value: Registration is annual and expires on 31 December of the registration year.

value.action: Create and activate an account on the eGP portal.
Sign in, open supplier registration and complete the business profile.
Upload the required company and ownership documents in PDF format.
Select the appropriate supplier category or categories and submit the registration.
Pay the applicable category fee through the portal and await PRAZ verification and review.
Resolve any returned profile issues, then download the certificate after approval.

notes: Registration and fees are category-specific; recheck the live eGP portal before payment.

name: Harare Business Licence Guide

whoNeedsIt.value: Retail, wholesale, food and other businesses falling within a licence class on Harare's business-licence application.

value: Completed Harare application for a shop licence, municipal licence or food-purveyors licence for the applicable class
documents.note: The form and the activity determine whether additional supporting material is requested.

value: Premises suitable for the proposed business use
Compliance with the sanitation, lighting, ventilation, surfaces, storage and waste controls applicable to the activity
Additional food-premises and food-handler health controls where food is handled

officialFees.note: A final current fee for the applicant's licence class was not verified; the reviewed 2026 tariff document was presented as proposed budget tariffs.

processingTime.note: No current council-published end-to-end processing time was located.

renewalCycle.value: The City of Harare describes business-licence fees as annual and provides a renewal route.
renewalCycle.note: Confirm the operative expiry date and renewal window for the licence class.

value.action: Identify the correct shop, municipal or food-purveyors licence class for the activity.
Prepare the premises against the council checklist and obtain any activity-specific approvals.
Complete the council application form for the premises and person in control.
Submit the application for the applicable health inspection and council approval.
Pay the authority-confirmed fee and obtain the licence before relying on it for trading.

notes: Licence class and premises requirements vary by activity; confirm the final tariff and inspection route with the council.

name: Harare Health Registration of Business Premises Guide

whoNeedsIt.value: Business premises falling within a class listed on Harare's health-registration application.

value: Completed Health Registration of Business Premises application form
Premises that meet the sanitation, structure, ventilation, storage and waste controls applicable to the activity
Identification of the applicant, trading name, premises and person in control

officialFees.note: No final current health-registration fee for each premises class was verified.

processingTime.note: No current council-published processing duration was located.

renewalCycle.note: The official form supports renewal but does not establish the renewal cadence.

value.action: Identify the premises class and whether the filing is new, renewal or change of ownership.
Prepare the premises against the council's applicable health and structural controls.
Complete and submit the health-registration form with the applicant, premises and person-in-control details.
Make the premises available for Environmental Health Officer assessment.
Address any official findings and obtain the City Health approval and registration.

notes: The form covers multiple premises classes; confirm the operative fee and renewal rule with City Health.

name: Bulawayo Shop Licence Guide

whoNeedsIt.value: Retail or wholesale businesses operating from a shop, store or other fixed premises in Bulawayo.

value: Completed trading-permit application for a new applicant
Current ZIMRA tax-clearance certificate
Valid trading permit and enabling certificate for the full licence
Original prior-year shop licence for renewal
An Environmental Health Officer site inspection for the trading-permit stage
Council consideration and the enabling certificate required for the full shop licence

officialFees.note: The council page displays fee ranges without a sufficiently clear current currency and class mapping for safe reuse.

processingTime.note: A council-meeting stage is published, but no complete application turnaround is stated.

renewalCycle.value: The shop licence is renewed annually.

value.action: Submit the trading-permit application for the proposed premises and activity.
Make the site available for Environmental Health inspection and resolve any findings.
Obtain the approved trading permit and submit the full shop-licence requirements.
Complete the council consideration, notice and enabling-certificate stages identified by the council.
Pay the authority-confirmed fee and collect the issued shop licence.

notes: The temporary trading permit and full shop licence are distinct stages; confirm current fees with the council.

name: Bulawayo Registration Certificate for Business Premises Guide

whoNeedsIt.value: Registrable premises identified by the Bulawayo Health Inspectorate, including food premises, hairdressers, hotels, lodging premises, laundries and dry cleaners.

value: Town-planning approval where required for the proposed use
Approved building plan
Payment of the council inspection fee confirmed by the authority
A satisfactory Environmental Health Officer inspection

officialFees.note: The official page requires an inspection fee but does not publish the current amount.

processingTime.note: No end-to-end processing duration is stated on the reviewed official page.

renewalCycle.value: The certificate is valid until 31 December and must be renewed annually.

value.action: Confirm with Environmental Health that the premises and activity require registration.
Obtain town-planning approval where required and an approved building plan.
Pay the authority-confirmed inspection fee and arrange the Environmental Health inspection.
Resolve inspection findings and apply for the registration certificate after the premises passes.
Renew the certificate annually before its current term ends.

notes: Premises standards vary by activity; obtain activity-specific direction from Environmental Health.

name: Zimbabwe Liquor Licence Guide

whoNeedsIt.value: Applicants seeking authority to sell liquor under the licence classes administered under Zimbabwe's Liquor Act.

value: Proof of payment of the applicable application fee
Premises plans approved by the Environmental Health Officer
Police report
Proof of citizenship for the applicant and proposed manager
Lease, title deed or other proof of the right to occupy the premises
Vetted fingerprints
Required notice in the Government Gazette and a local newspaper
Premises suitable for the proposed licence and consistent with applicable zoning
An applicant and proposed manager who meet the Board's suitability and character requirements
Selection of the correct liquor-licence class

officialFees.note: The official service page requires payment but does not publish a current amount for each licence class.

processingTime.note: No current end-to-end processing time was verified on the reviewed official service page.

renewalCycle.note: The reviewed service page does not establish the term or renewal cycle for each licence class.

officialLinks.note: The Board portal was unavailable during review, so it is not presented as a verified filing link.

steps.note: The official page publishes eligibility and document requirements but not an authoritative ordered application sequence.

notes: This is a national overview; confirm class-specific filing, notice, fee and renewal instructions with the Liquor Licensing Board.

name: Environmental and Social Impact Assessment Certificate Guide

whoNeedsIt.value: Developers of projects in EMA-listed sectors that may cause significant adverse environmental effects.

value: Project prospectus covering the project, location, baseline environment, anticipated impacts, mitigation measures, project cost, terms of reference and legal framework
Full ESIA report prepared by an EMA-registered consultant when EMA requires a full assessment
Six copies of the ESIA report for review when submitting the full report
Screen the project against EMA's prescribed sectors before implementation
Use an EMA-registered consultant when a full ESIA is required

value.description: ESIA review fee, selected by EMA's published project-cost band
value.taxTreatment: Not stated on the cited page

value.currency: USD equivalent payable in ZiG at the prevailing interbank rate
value.taxTreatment: Not stated on the cited page

officialFees.note: Confirm the project-cost band, payment currency and current amount with EMA before payment.

processingTime.value: EMA states 60 working days for ESIA review and at least one month for renewal processing.

renewalCycle.value: The certificate is valid for two years. If the project is not complete, renewal must be initiated six months before expiry; EMA states a one-year extension after renewal.

value.action: Screen the proposed project against EMA's listed sectors before implementation.
Prepare and submit the project prospectus with the required project, baseline, impact, mitigation, cost and legal-framework information.
If EMA requires a full ESIA, appoint a registered consultant and prepare the full report.
Submit the required report copies and pay the authority-confirmed review fee.
Do not implement the prescribed project before EMA issues the certificate.
Submit monitoring reports and start renewal six months before expiry if the project remains incomplete.

notes: Applicability and the assessment depth are project-specific; obtain EMA confirmation before implementation.

name: Effluent Licence Guide

whoNeedsIt.value: Enterprises, industrial operations and mining operations that discharge effluent.

value: Certificate of incorporation
Company profile
Identification document for the company manager or director
Current tax-clearance certificate
Local-council endorsement where effluent is discharged into a council reticulation system
Completed EMA effluent application form
A site inspection by EMA
Laboratory test results for industrial and mining effluent applications
Council endorsement where the discharge enters a council system

officialFees.note: No current fee amount was stated on the reviewed official page or form.

processingTime.note: No authority-published end-to-end processing duration was located.

renewalCycle.value: Valid for one calendar year and expiring on 31 December; renewal is required for continued licensed discharge activity.
renewalCycle.note: The previously reviewed official sources did not publish the licence term or renewal cadence. PB-002B located EMA's one-calendar-year validity and 31 December expiry statement.

value.action: Complete the EMA effluent application and assemble the company, identity and tax-clearance documents.
Obtain local-council endorsement if the discharge enters a council reticulation system.
Submit the application and make the site available for EMA inspection.
For industrial or mining effluent, obtain and submit the laboratory test information required by EMA.
Address EMA findings and obtain the licence before relying on it for discharge.

notes: Requirements differ between enterprise and industrial or mining discharges; confirm classification with EMA.

name: Electricity Generation Licence Guide

whoNeedsIt.value: Electricity undertakings generating, transmitting, distributing or supplying electricity in excess of 100 kW; this guide covers generation applications.

value: Completed generation-licence application form
Business plan and company registration documents
Board profile and evidence of financing and technical capacity
Current tax-clearance certificate
Grid Impact Assessment, proposed power-purchase arrangements and applicable fuel-supply arrangements
Bankable feasibility study and project implementation schedule
Land-use permit, project-location maps and applicable water-extraction permit
EMA environmental-impact-assessment certificate
Proof of payment of the application fee
documents.note: The detailed requirement set depends on whether generation supplies the grid, own consumption or identified customers.

value: Determine the generation model and plant to be licensed
Obtain the applicable land, environmental and network studies and approvals
Demonstrate financing and technical capability for the project

value.taxTreatment: Excluding VAT

value.description: Non-renewable commercial plant up to 10 MW
value.taxTreatment: Excluding VAT

value.amount: 10,000 fixed plus 5,000 per 25 MW or part thereof
value.taxTreatment: Excluding VAT
Excluding VAT

officialFees.note: Renewable commercial licence fees vary by technology and capacity; consult the current ZERA table and subsequent amendments before payment.

processingTime.value: No more than six months from ZERA's receipt of the application and all supporting documents to notice of its decision or proposed decision, unless the applicant consents to an extension.
processingTime.note: The previously reviewed ZERA form and guidelines did not state an end-to-end determination time. PB-002B located the statutory six-month ceiling and extension condition in the Electricity Act.

renewalCycle.value: ZERA determines the licence term, subject to a maximum of 30 years; the holder may apply for renewal before expiry.
renewalCycle.note: The previously reviewed guidelines publish renewal fees but do not state one general licence term for every generation licence. PB-002B located the statutory maximum term and renewal route in the Electricity Act.

value.action: Determine the plant and generation model, then download the current form and requirements.
Complete the project, corporate, technical, financial, network and environmental evidence applicable to the model.
Pay the authority-confirmed application fee and retain proof.
Email the completed form and all requirements to the ZERA electricity-licensing address stated in the current guidance.
Resolve any completeness or assessment issues raised by ZERA before the licence is determined.

notes: Generation requirements and licence fees depend on plant type, capacity and supply model; revalidate current amendments with ZERA.

name: Petroleum Retail Licence Guide

whoNeedsIt.value: A person or company operating a site-specific petroleum retail activity in Zimbabwe.

value: Completed site-specific petroleum retail application form
Local-authority permit or licence
Fire Brigade certificate
EMA Hazardous Substance Storage and Use Licence
Certified Certificate of Incorporation, CR14, CR6 and CR2 or share certificates
Certified title deeds or lease agreement for the retail site
Certified copy of the applicant's identity document
A compliant retail site with local-authority, fire and EMA approvals
A separate application for each site

value.taxTreatment: Excluding VAT
Excluding VAT

officialFees.note: Confirm the site's classification and any subsequent fee amendment with ZERA before payment.

processingTime.value: No more than six months from ZERA's receipt of the complete application and supporting documents to its final decision, unless the applicant consents to an extension.
processingTime.note: No end-to-end processing duration was stated in the previously reviewed petroleum sources. PB-002B located the statutory six-month ceiling and extension condition in the Energy Regulatory Authority Act.

renewalCycle.value: ZERA states that petroleum licences are valid for one year and renewable after satisfactory compliance audits.

value.action: Obtain the local-authority, Fire Brigade and EMA approvals for the retail site.
Complete a separate petroleum retail application for the site.
Attach the certified company, identity, occupation-right and site-approval documents in the checklist.
Pay the authority-confirmed rural or urban fee and submit the complete application to ZERA.
Address ZERA assessment or inspection findings and obtain the licence before retailing.

notes: The application is site-specific; confirm site classification, audit readiness and current fees with ZERA.

name: Liquefied Petroleum Gas Retail Licence Guide

whoNeedsIt.value: A person or company carrying on LPG retail business in Zimbabwe.

value: Completed ER2 LPG retail application form
Certified applicant identity document
Certified CR14, CR6, CR2 and Certificate of Incorporation
Lease agreement or title deeds
EMA certificate
Fire Brigade certificate
Local-authority permit
Local-authority planning, fire and environmental approvals for the site
EMA assessment and certificate
A compliant LPG retail site and right to occupy it

value.taxTreatment: Excluding VAT

officialFees.note: Confirm any subsequent statutory amendment with ZERA before payment.

processingTime.value: No more than six months from ZERA's receipt of the complete application and supporting documents to its final decision, unless the applicant consents to an extension.
processingTime.note: No end-to-end processing duration was stated in the previously reviewed LPG sources. PB-002B located the statutory six-month ceiling and extension condition in the Energy Regulatory Authority Act.

renewalCycle.value: An LPG licence is valid for a period not exceeding two calendar years and may be renewed under the regulations.
renewalCycle.note: A renewal form existed in the previously reviewed sources, but those sources did not establish the term. PB-002B located the maximum two-calendar-year term in the LPG regulations.

value.action: Obtain local-authority planning, fire and environmental approvals for the LPG site.
Complete the EMA stage and obtain the required EMA certificate.
Complete the ZERA ER2 application for the retail site.
Attach the identity, company, occupation-right, EMA, fire and local-authority documents.
Pay the authority-confirmed fee, submit to ZERA and resolve any assessment findings before retailing.

notes: The licensing sequence has local-authority, EMA and ZERA gates; confirm the current term and fee before filing.

name: Unified Telecommunications Services Licence Guide

whoNeedsIt.value: Telecommunications operators seeking the umbrella unified licence covering the converged Network Facilities, Network Services and Application Services categories.

documents.note: No current unified-licence application checklist was located in the reviewed official sources.

prerequisites.note: The official category guidance describes licence scope but does not publish a complete prerequisite set for an application.

value.taxTreatment: Not stated in the cited schedule

value.description: Unified Telecommunications Services initial or renewal fee
value.taxTreatment: Not stated in the cited schedule
VAT applies as stated

officialFees.note: Confirm the current category, fee schedule and payment instructions directly with POTRAZ before committing funds.

processingTime.note: No authority-published application turnaround was located in the reviewed official sources.

renewalCycle.value: The published tenure for the Unified Telecommunications Services Licence is 20 years; POTRAZ lists an initial or renewal fee and separate annual charges.

steps.note: The reviewed official documents do not provide a current ordered filing sequence for the unified licence.

notes: POTRAZ offers multiple converged subcategories; confirm whether the umbrella unified licence is the correct authorisation before applying.

name: Medicines Premises Licence Guide

whoNeedsIt.value: Premises used as pharmacies, dispensing medical or veterinary practices, manufacturers, clinics and other medicines premises subject to MCAZ licensing.

value: Completed premises-licence application form submitted in triplicate
Approved premises plans and floor-layout plan
Local-authority approval for use or change of use and Environmental Health approval
Proof of citizenship or ordinary residence for the individual applicant or the majority of company directors, unless exempted
Memorandum and articles of association and current company-director particulars where the applicant is a company
Affidavits or police clearances concerning relevant convictions for company directors
Applicable professional-council confirmation for the premises type
Proof of payment of the applicable application fee
documents.note: The exact checklist varies for pharmacies, manufacturers, dispensing practitioners and clinics.

value: Premises approved for the intended use by the local authority
A qualifying supervisor and any professional-council approvals applicable to the premises type
Premises ready for MCAZ inspection against the applicable checklist

value.description: Pharmacy in the central business district of a city
value.taxTreatment: Not stated in the cited schedule
Not stated in the cited schedule

value.description: Pharmacy under a rural district council
value.taxTreatment: Not stated in the cited schedule

value.description: Dispensing medical practitioner or veterinary surgeon premises
value.taxTreatment: Not stated in the cited schedule
Not stated in the cited schedule

value.description: Dispensary at a local-authority or public-health clinic
value.taxTreatment: Not stated in the cited schedule

officialFees.note: The cited fee schedule took effect on 1 September 2025; confirm the correct premises class and any later circular before payment.

processingTime.note: MCAZ publishes a 60-day window to resolve outstanding application issues, but that is not an end-to-end determination time.

renewalCycle.value: A premises licence is valid for 12 months and is renewed using the applicable MCAZ renewal route.
renewalCycle.note: Commencement dates differ by premises category under the regulations.

value.action: Identify the premises class and download its current application and inspection checklist.
Obtain local-authority use, plan and Environmental Health approvals and the applicable professional-council confirmations.
Complete the premises application and assemble the applicant, company, character, premises and payment evidence.
Submit the application through the current MCAZ route and make the premises available for inspection.
Resolve outstanding issues within the MCAZ-stated response window and obtain the licence before operating the regulated premises.

notes: MCAZ requirements, inspection checklists and commencement dates vary by premises type; use the current class-specific material.
